solve(s) ->
    case build_frequencies(s) of
        {FreqMap, UniqueChars} ->
            process_unique_chars(FreqMap, UniqueChars, 0, [])
    end.

%% Helper function to build frequency map and list of unique characters
build_frequencies(List) ->
    FrequencyMap = lists:foldl(count_char, #{}, List),
    UniqueChars = lists:keys(FrequencyMap),
    {FrequencyMap, UniqueChars}.

%% Accumulator for building frequency map
count_char(Map, Char) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Count, _){Count + 1}; 0, Map#{Char => 0}, Char).

%% Main processing function: processes unique characters iteratively
%% process_unique_chars(FreqMap, RemainingChars, TotalDifference, ProcessedCharsSet)
process_unique_chars(_Map, [], Total, _Processed) ->
    Total;
process_unique_chars(FreqMap, [C|Rest], Total, Processed) ->
    if lists:member(C, Processed) ->
        process_unique_chars(FreqMap, Rest, Total, Processed);
    true ->
        M = get_mirror(C),
        
        %% If M is not valid (shouldn't happen based on constraints), skip
        if M == null ->
            process_unique_chars(FreqMap, Rest, Total, Processed);
        true ->
            FreqC = maps:get(C, FreqMap, 0),
            FreqM = maps:get(M, FreqMap, 0),
            
            Diff = abs(FreqC - FreqM),
            NewTotal = Total + Diff,
            
            %% Mark both C and M as processed
            NewProcessed = lists:insert(C, lists:insert(M, Processed)),
            
            process_unique_chars(FreqMap, Rest, NewTotal, NewProcessed)
        end
    end.

%% Helper function to determine the mirror character
get_mirror(Char) ->
    Code = char_to_code(Char),
    
    %% Lowercase letter check ('a'=97, 'z'=122)
    if Code >= 97 and Code =< 122 ->
        Base = 97,
        P = Code - Base,
        P_Prime = 25 - P,
        Code_Mirror = Base + P_Prime,
        char_to_string(Code_Mirror)
    
    %% Digit check ('0'=48, '9'=57)
    elseif Code >= 48 and Code =< 57 ->
        Base = 48,
        P = Code - Base,
        P_Prime = 9 - P,
        Code_Mirror = Base + P_Prime,
        char_to_string(Code_Mirror)
        
    %% Fallback for unexpected characters (should not happen)
    else
        null
    end.
```

fun solve(Str) -> integer() ->
    % Step 1: Frequency Counting
    FreqMap = map_from_list(Str),

    % Helper function to get the mirror character (returns code point or none)
    get_mirror(Code) when Code >= 97 and Code =< 122 ->
        % Lowercase letters 'a' (97) to 'z' (122)
        MirrorCode = 2 * 97 + 25 - Code,
        MirrorCode;
    get_mirror(Code) when Code >= 48 and Code =< 57 ->
        % Digits '0' (48) to '9' (57)
        MirrorCode = 2 * 48 + 9 - Code,
        MirrorCode;
    get_mirror(_) ->
        none end,

    % Get all unique character codes present in the string
    UniqueCodes = map_keys(FreqMap),

    % Step 2 & 3: Iterating and Summing
    % We use a recursive helper to process pairs and maintain a processed set
    calculate_sum(UniqueCodes, FreqMap, [] =:= [], TotalSum) ->
        TotalSum;
    calculate_sum([C | Rest], FreqMap, Processed, TotalSum) ->
        case check_processed(C, Processed) of
            true ->
                calculate_sum(Rest, FreqMap, Processed, TotalSum);
            false ->
                % Character C needs processing
                MirrorCode = get_mirror(C),
                
                case MirrorCode of
                    none ->
                        % Should not happen based on constraints
                        calculate_sum(Rest, FreqMap, [C | Processed], TotalSum);
                    MCode ->
                        % Ensure MCode is also present in the unique list for completeness, 
                        % although we only need to process the pair once based on C.
                        
                        % Calculate frequencies (0 if not present)
                        FreqC = maps:get(C, FreqMap, 0),
                        FreqM = maps:get(MCode, FreqMap, 0),
                        
                        % Calculate absolute difference
                        Diff = abs(FreqC - FreqM),
                        
                        % Update processed set (C and MCode)
                        NewProcessed = [C | MCode | Processed],
                        
                        % Recurse
                        calculate_sum(Rest, FreqMap, NewProcessed, TotalSum + Diff)
                end
        end end.

    % Helper to check if a code has been processed
    check_processed(Code, Processed) ->
        lists:member(Code, Processed) end,

    % Start the calculation
    calculate_sum(UniqueCodes, FreqMap, [], 0)
end.

% --- Auxiliary Functions ---

% Converts binary string to frequency map: code -> count
map_from_list(Str) ->
    % Convert binary string to list of byte values (integers)
    List = binary_to_list(Str),
    
    % Use lists:foldl to build the map
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Code) ->
        maps:update_with(fun(Count) -> Count + 1 end, Code, Acc)
    end, #{}, List)
end.

fun solve(Str) -> integer() ->
    % Step 1: Frequency Counting
    FreqMap = map_from_list(Str),

    % Helper function to get the mirror character (returns code point or none)
    get_mirror(Code) when Code >= 97 and Code =< 122 ->
        % Lowercase letters 'a' (97) to 'z' (122)
        MirrorCode = 2 * 97 + 25 - Code,
        MirrorCode;
    get_mirror(Code) when Code >= 48 and Code =< 57 ->
        % Digits '0' (48) to '9' (57)
        MirrorCode = 2 * 48 + 9 - Code,
        MirrorCode;
    get_mirror(_) ->
        none end,

    % Get all unique character codes present in the string
    UniqueCodes = map_keys(FreqMap),

    % Step 2 & 3: Iterating and Summing
    % We use a recursive helper to process pairs and maintain a processed set
    calculate_sum(UniqueCodes, FreqMap, [] =:= [], TotalSum) ->
        TotalSum;
    calculate_sum([C | Rest], FreqMap, Processed, TotalSum) ->
        case check_processed(C, Processed) of
            true ->
                calculate_sum(Rest, FreqMap, Processed, TotalSum);
            false ->
                % Character C needs processing
                MirrorCode = get_mirror(C),
                
                case MirrorCode of
                    none ->
                        % Should not happen based on constraints
                        calculate_sum(Rest, FreqMap, [C | Processed], TotalSum);
                    MCode ->
                        % Ensure MCode is also present in the unique list for completeness, 
                        % although we only need to process the pair once based on C.
                        
                        % Calculate frequencies (0 if not present)
                        FreqC = maps:get(C, FreqMap, 0),
                        FreqM = maps:get(MCode, FreqMap, 0),
                        
                        % Calculate absolute difference
                        Diff = abs(FreqC - FreqM),
                        
                        % Update processed set (C and MCode)
                        NewProcessed = [C | MCode | Processed],
                        
                        % Recurse
                        calculate_sum(Rest, FreqMap, NewProcessed, TotalSum + Diff)
                end
        end end.

    % Helper to check if a code has been processed
    check_processed(Code, Processed) ->
        lists:member(Code, Processed) end,

    % Start the calculation
    calculate_sum(UniqueCodes, FreqMap, [], 0)
end.

% --- Auxiliary Functions ---

% Converts binary string to frequency map: code -> count
map_from_list(Str) ->
    % Convert binary string to list of byte values (integers)
    List = binary_to_list(Str),
    
    % Use lists:foldl to build the map
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Code) ->
        maps:update_with(fun(Count) -> Count + 1 end, Code, Acc)
    end, #{}, List)
end.

% Helper to convert a list of byte values back to a binary string (used conceptually in the JS version, 
% but here we work with integer codes directly in Erlang for efficiency)
% binary_to_list/1 is available in standard Erlang libraries.

-spec solve(binary()) -> integer().
solve(S) ->
    % Step 1: Frequency Counting
    % FreqMap: {Code, Count}
    FreqMap = count_frequencies(S),

    % Step 2: Get unique character codes
    % We extract all unique codes present in S.
    AllCodes = get_unique_codes(S),

    % Step 3: Iterating and Summing
    TotalSum = sum_differences(AllCodes, FreqMap),
    TotalSum.

%% Helper: Counts frequencies of bytes (characters) in the binary string S.
count_frequencies(S) ->
    foldl(increment_frequency, [], S).

increment_frequency(FreqMap, Byte) ->
    case lookup(Byte, FreqMap) of
        {Byte, Count} ->
            lists:replace(FreqMap, {Byte, Count + 1}),
        no_match ->
            [{Byte, 1} | FreqMap]
    end.

%% Helper: Extracts unique byte codes from the binary string S.
get_unique_codes(S) ->
    % Extract all bytes and then get unique ones
    list_to_binary(S, CodesBinary), % Should just return the bytes list if S is bytes
    % Since S is a binary, we convert it to a list of bytes (integers 0-255)
    ByteList = binary_to_list(S),
    lists:unique(ByteList).

%% Helper: Calculates the sum of absolute differences across all unique pairs.
sum_differences(AllCodes, FreqMap) ->
    % We need to track processed codes to avoid double counting {c, m} and {m, c}
    % We use a tuple {Sum, ProcessedCodes}
    {TotalSum, _ProcessedCodes} = foldl(calculate_diff, {0, []}, AllCodes),
    TotalSum.

calculate_diff({CurrentSum, ProcessedCodes}, CodeC) when is_integer(CodeC) ->
    case member(CodeC, ProcessedCodes) of
        true ->
            % Already processed as part of another pair
            {CurrentSum, ProcessedCodes};
        false ->
            CodeM = get_mirror(CodeC),
            
            % Check if mirror calculation resulted in a valid code
            case CodeM of
                none ->
                    % Should not happen based on constraints
                    {CurrentSum, [CodeC | ProcessedCodes]};
                CodeM_Valid ->
                    % Get frequencies. If not found, frequency is 0.
                    FreqC = get_frequency(FreqMap, CodeC),
                    FreqM = get_frequency(FreqMap, CodeM_Valid),
                    
                    % Calculate absolute difference
                    Diff = abs(FreqC - FreqM),
                    
                    NewSum = CurrentSum + Diff,
                    % Mark both C and M as processed
                    NewProcessed = [CodeC, CodeM_Valid | ProcessedCodes],
                    
                    {NewSum, NewProcessed}
            end.

%% Helper: Retrieves frequency for a given code, defaults to 0.
get_frequency(FreqMap, Code) ->
    case lookup(Code, FreqMap) of
        {_Code, Count} -> Count;
        no_match -> 0
    end.

%% Helper: Calculates the mirrored ASCII code.
%% Returns the code (integer) or 'none' if the character is invalid.
get_mirror(Code) when is_integer(Code) ->
    % Check for lowercase letters: 97 ('a') to 122 ('z')
    if Code >= 97 and Code =< 122 ->
        BaseLetter = 97,
        % Position p (0 to 25)
        P = Code - BaseLetter,
        % Mirror position p' = 25 - p
        P_Prime = 25 - P,
        % New code

        % Position p (0 to 9)
        P = Code - BaseDigit,
        % Mirror position p' = 9 - p
        P_Prime = 9 - P,
        % New code
        CodeC = BaseDigit + P_Prime,
        CodeC;

    true ->
        none % Invalid character code
    end.

-spec solve(binary()) -> integer().
solve(S) ->
    % Step 1: Frequency Counting
    % FreqMap: {Code, Count}
    FreqMap = count_frequencies(S),

    % Step 2: Get unique character codes
    % We extract all unique codes present in S.
    AllCodes = get_unique_codes(S),

    % Step 3: Iterating and Summing
    TotalSum = sum_differences(AllCodes, FreqMap),
    TotalSum.

%% Helper: Counts frequencies of bytes (characters) in the binary string S.
count_frequencies(S) ->
    foldl(increment_frequency, [], S).

increment_frequency(FreqMap, Byte) ->
    case lookup(Byte, FreqMap) of
        {Byte, Count} ->
            lists:replace(FreqMap, {Byte, Count + 1}),
        no_match ->
            [{Byte, 1} | FreqMap]
    end.

%% Helper: Extracts unique byte codes from the binary string S.
get_unique_codes(S) ->
    % Extract all bytes and then get unique ones
    list_to_binary(S, CodesBinary), % Should just return the bytes list if S is bytes
    % Since S is a binary, we convert it to a list of bytes (integers 0-255)
    ByteList = binary_to_list(S),
    lists:unique(ByteList).

%% Helper: Calculates the sum of absolute differences across all unique pairs.
sum_differences(AllCodes, FreqMap) ->
    % We need to track processed codes to avoid double counting {c, m} and {m, c}
    % We use a tuple {Sum, ProcessedCodes}
    {TotalSum, _ProcessedCodes} = foldl(calculate_diff, {0, []}, AllCodes),
    TotalSum.

calculate_diff({CurrentSum, ProcessedCodes}, CodeC) when is_integer(CodeC) ->
    case member(CodeC, ProcessedCodes) of
        true ->
            % Already processed as part of another pair
            {CurrentSum, ProcessedCodes};
        false ->
            CodeM = get_mirror(CodeC),
            
            % Check if mirror calculation resulted in a valid code
            case CodeM of
                none ->
                    % Should not happen based on constraints
                    {CurrentSum, [CodeC | ProcessedCodes]};
                CodeM_Valid ->
                    % Get frequencies. If not found, frequency is 0.
                    FreqC = get_frequency(FreqMap, CodeC),
                    FreqM = get_frequency(FreqMap, CodeM_Valid),
                    
                    % Calculate absolute difference
                    Diff = abs(FreqC - FreqM),
                    
                    NewSum = CurrentSum + Diff,
                    % Mark both C and M as processed
                    NewProcessed = [CodeC, CodeM_Valid | ProcessedCodes],
                    
                    {NewSum, NewProcessed}
            end.

%% Helper: Retrieves frequency for a given code, defaults to 0.
get_frequency(FreqMap, Code) ->
    case lookup(Code, FreqMap) of
        {_Code, Count} -> Count;
        no_match -> 0
    end.

%% Helper: Calculates the mirrored ASCII code.
%% Returns the code (integer) or 'none' if the character is invalid.
get_mirror(Code) when is_integer(Code) ->
    % Check for lowercase letters: 97 ('a') to 122 ('z')
    if Code >= 97 and Code =< 122 ->
        BaseLetter = 97,
        % Position p (0 to 25)
        P = Code - BaseLetter,
        % Mirror position p' = 25 - p
        P_Prime = 25 - P,
        % New code
        CodeC = BaseLetter + P_Prime,
        CodeC;

    % Check for digits: 48 ('0') to 57 ('9')
    elseif Code >= 48 and Code =< 57 ->
        BaseDigit = 48,
        % Position p (0 to 9)
        P = Code - BaseDigit,
        % Mirror position p' = 9 - p
        P_Prime = 9 - P,
        % New code
        CodeC = BaseDigit + P_Prime,
        CodeC;

    true ->
        none % Invalid character code
    end.

    lookup_freq(Char, FreqMap) ->

    get_mirror(Char) ->

    list_to_map(Bytes, #{}).

% Helper function to accumulate frequencies
list_to_map([], Map) -> Map;
list_to_map([Byte | Rest], Map) ->
    list_to_map(Rest, maps:update_count(Byte, Map)).

maps:update_count(Byte, Map) ->
    maps:update(Byte, fun(Count) -> Count + 1 end, Map).

% Helper function to get the mirror character (byte value)
get_mirror(Byte) when is_integer(Byte) ->
    case Byte of
        B when B >= 97 and B =< 122 -> % Lowercase letter 'a' (97) to 'z' (122)
            % Position p (0 for 'a')
            P is B - 97,
            % Mirror position p' = 25 - p
            P_prime is 25 - P,
            % New byte value
            NewByte is 97 + P_prime,
            NewByte;

        B when B >= 48 and B =< 57 -> % Digit '0' (48) to '9' (57)
            % Position p (0 for '0')
            P is B - 48,
            % Mirror position p' = 9 - p
            P_prime is 9 - P,
            % New byte value
            NewByte is 48 + P_prime,
            NewByte;

        _ ->
            % Should not happen based on constraints
            none
    end.

% Helper function to count frequencies of bytes in a binary string
count_frequencies(Binary) ->
    binary_to_list(Binary, Bytes),
    list_to_map(Bytes, #{}).

% Helper function to accumulate frequencies
list_to_map([], Map) -> Map;
list_to_map([Byte | Rest], Map) ->
    list_to_map(Rest, maps:update_count(Byte, Map)).

maps:update_count(Byte, Map) ->
    maps:update(Byte, fun(Count) -> Count + 1 end, Map).

% Main solver function
solve(S) ->
    % 1. Frequency Counting
    Frequencies = count_frequencies(S),
    
    % 2. Identify unique characters (bytes)
    BytesList = binary_to_list(S),
    UniqueBytes = maps:keys(maps:fold(fun(B, M) -> maps:insert(B, true, M) end, #{}, BytesList)),

    % 3. Iteration and Summing using fold/recursion
    TotalDiff = sum_diff(UniqueBytes, Frequencies, #{}, 0).

    TotalDiff.

% Recursive helper to calculate the sum
% Args: [Remaining Unique Bytes, Frequencies Map, Processed Bytes Set, Current Sum]
sum_diff([], _, _, Sum) -> Sum;
sum_diff([C | Rest], Freqs, Processed, Sum) ->
    case maps:member(C, Processed) of
        true ->
            % Already processed this pair
            sum_diff(Rest, Freqs, Processed, Sum);
        false ->
            % Process character C
            Mirror = get_mirror(C),
            
            case Mirror of
                none -> % Should not happen
                    sum_diff(Rest, Freqs, Processed, Sum);
                
                M ->
                    % Get frequencies, defaulting to 0 if not present
                    FreqC = maps:get(C, Freqs, 0),
                    FreqM = maps:get(M, Freqs, 0),
                    
                    % Calculate difference
                    Diff = abs(FreqC - FreqM),
                    
                    % Update state
                    NewProcessed = maps:insert(C, true, maps:insert(M, true, Processed)),
                    NewSum = Sum + Diff,
                    
                    % Continue iteration
                    sum_diff(Rest, Freqs, NewProcessed, NewSum)
            end.
